competitive_position.summary: Stemline is evaluating Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis, and Ironclad alongside Zycus. No competitive intelligence on which competitor is leading or how Zycus compares. The wedge is Merlin AI negotiation agents with Google Gemini integration for indirect services procurement (pharma-specific use case) plus referenceable biotech/pharma customers. Both wedges are at risk: the two demo commitments (Google Gemini confirmation, biotech references) remain unresolved 8 months post-demo. If Zycus cannot confirm Google Gemini compatibility or provide biotech references, the deal is exposed.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08; SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator); SF Next_Step 2026-07-01
champion_strength.summary: Liz Colucci (Director, Vendor & Sourcing) is the identified champion. She was actively engaged on the 2025-10-08 discovery call, shaped the demo requirements, and has direct access to the economic buyer. However, she has been passive since the 2025-10-27 demo (8 months ago) and has not proactively pushed the evaluation forward, requested pricing, or provided post-demo feedback. Rep reached out to her on 2026-07-01 (today) for feedback; awaiting reply. Risk: competing priorities, unresolved demo blockers (Google Gemini confirmation, biotech references), or budget uncertainty may be stalling her advocacy.

sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08: economic buyer outlined decision process, budget cycle miss, 2026 timing TBD
SF Next_Step_History__c 2026-06-10: rep encounter at DPW NY, economic buyer mentioned 'project back on table'
No pricing request or business case activity logged in 8 months post-demo
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer controlled the commercial and pricing conversation on the 2025-10-08 discovery call. He outlined a 3-step decision process (gather info, obtain pricing and assemble business case, seek budget approval) and stated that Stemline missed the 2025 budget cycle (evaluation began Sept/Oct 2025; annual budgeting is summer/fall) and timing for 2026 budget approval is 'TBD.' He also said 'It's probably not gonna be for your year end target,' indicating he holds budget authority and the 2026 budget window is uncertain. He has not re-engaged since the demo (8 months ago), but a June 2026 Next Step notes he mentioned the 'project back on table' at a booth encounter (DPW NY event), suggesting passive sponsorship but no active commercial follow-through. His absence from any pricing or business-case discussion in the last 8 months signals the deal is on hold pending budget clarity or resolution of the two demo blockers (Google Gemini confirmation, biotech references).

recommended_moves: Deliver the two unresolved demo commitments in one package: (1) confirm all Merlin agents work with the relevant stakeholder (not Azure OpenAI) with technical documentation, (2) provide biotech/pharma references for indirect services CLM with case studies and offer to arrange customer call. Send to champion (Liz Colucci) and copy the economic buyer. Position as 'closing the loop from the October demo' and request a follow-up call to discuss next steps.

Request a follow-up call with champion (Liz Colucci) and the economic buyer to confirm post-demo feedback, discuss the two deliverables (Google Gemini confirmation, biotech references), and clarify the 2026 budget approval window and business case timeline. Position as 'understanding where the project stands now that it's back on the table.'

Executive connect: have the deal owner's manager reach out to the economic buyer to confirm the project is back on the table, discuss the 2026 budget approval window, and offer to accelerate the business case assembly with ROI modeling or a workshop. Position as 'executive-level support to help Stemline hit the 2026 budget window.'
